page: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU virtual server powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.91 GB per core, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. No GPU resources or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate a distinct performance profile. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 3074.79, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 13.69 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance present significant tradeoffs. The stress-ng multi-core score of 3074.79 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2281.0 are weak, reflecting the physical single-core limitation. Memory performance is also constrained, with Passmark memory scores and database operations falling into the bottom 10%, and read-only memory bandwidth categorized as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ccx13 offers dedicated CPU resources at an entry-level scale, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the virtualization overhead of shared-core instances. However, the low memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling limit its utility for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, basic text processing, and low-traffic web serving where dedicated CPU execution is preferred over burstable alternatives.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local SSD storage, with no bundled GPU. The server exhibits strong single-core capabilities, achieving top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, it faces notable tradeoffs, including weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth. Qualitatively, the ccx13 provides a cost-effective entry point to dedicated CPU resources, avoiding the unpredictability of shared-core hosts. It is best suited for light general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU server with 2 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD. It offers top-tier single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx13 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 2 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 80 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server offering strong single-core performance and local SSD storage for light general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC Milan CPU with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth performance
Suited for light general-purpose workloads and single-threaded tasks
